Title: Expansion within the CYP71D subfamily drives the heterocyclization of tanshinones synthesis in Salvia miltiorrhiza

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: bh2-7-derived line from S. miltiorrhiza var. alba
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: D-ring heterocyclization branch of abietane diterpenoid/tanshinone pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms heterocyclic D-ring-containing tanshinone intermediate/product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive medicinal nor-diterpenoids with cardiovascular and cerebrovascular protective, antioxidant, antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, antitumor, and anti-HIV activities
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest expression in roots; expression profile similar to CYP76AH1/CYP76AH3/CYP76AK1
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C16 hydroxylation and 14,16-ether heterocyclization of Ro to form the characteristic D-ring-containing product 15,16-dihydrotanshinone I; also forms a 16-hydroxylated intermediate/product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for formation of characteristic D-ring, not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of the characteristic 14,16-epoxy/furan D-ring in tanshinones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown of clade followed by root color change and metabolite profiling; heterologous yeast expression and in vitro enzyme assays with authentic substrates/products; LC-MS/GC-MS metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ATR1 (Arabidopsis NADPH-CYP reductase in yeast assay context)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; metabolomics (LC-qTOF-MS, GC-QqQ-MS); genome sequencing/assembly BioProject PRJNA682867
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious phenotypic differences besides altered orange root coloration in CYP71Ds-RNAi plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71D373 is one of four root-expressed expanded CYP71D genes linked to tanshinone biosynthesis. 2. Recombinant CYP71D373 converted Ro (5) to 15,16-dihydrotanshinone I (2) and a 16-hydroxy derivative, demonstrating D-ring heterocyclization activity. 3. RNAi of the CYP71D clade reduced D-ring-containing tanshinones and increased non-cyclized intermediates, supporting its pathway role.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for metabolic engineering of tanshinone D-ring formation, especially in combination with upstream diterpene synthases and CYP76 enzymes; may be stacked with CYP71D375 to enhance flux to D-ring-containing tanshinones
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows partial functional redundancy with CYP71D375; authors suggest differential roles in inducible versus constitutive tanshinone production, but this remains speculative.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: bh2-7-derived line from S. miltiorrhiza var. alba
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: D-ring heterocyclization branch of abietane diterpenoid/tanshinone pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target heterocyclic tanshinone products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive medicinal nor-diterpenoids with cardiovascular and cerebrovascular protective, antioxidant, antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, antitumor, and anti-HIV activities
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest expression in roots; expression profile similar to known tanshinone biosynthetic genes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C16 hydroxylation and 14,16-ether heterocyclization to generate the characteristic D-ring of tanshinones, converting miltirone to cryptotanshinone and Ro to 15,16-dihydrotanshinone I; also acts on 4-methylenemiltirone
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for characteristic D-ring formation; not directly proven rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes formation of D-ring-containing tanshinones including cryptotanshinone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown of CYP71D clade with metabolite changes; heterologous yeast expression and in vitro enzyme assays; authentic standard comparison; NMR identification of products; substrate feeding of compound 8; site-directed mutagenesis with docking analysis
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ATR1 (Arabidopsis NADPH-CYP reductase in yeast assay context)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; metabolomics (LC-qTOF-MS, GC-QqQ-MS); genome sequencing/assembly BioProject PRJNA682867
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious gross phenotype reported beyond altered root pigmentation when the CYP71D clade was knocked down
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71D375 converts miltirone (4) to cryptotanshinone (1), with 16-hydroxymiltirone (7) and neocryptotanshinone (8) as minor products. 2. CYP71D375 also converts Ro (5) to 15,16-dihydrotanshinone I (2) and acts on 4-methylenemiltirone (6), indicating promiscuity and a metabolic grid. 3. Feeding neocryptotanshinone (8) did not yield cryptotanshinone, supporting direct heterocyclization from the 16-hydroxylated intermediate rather than dehydration. 4. Mutational analysis identified A301 and especially L366 as important for catalytic function.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime target for engineering production of D-ring-containing tanshinones and cryptotanshinone; should be stacked with upstream diterpene synthases and CYP76 enzymes to reconstruct/improve pathway flux
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors propose CYP71D375 may function more constitutively than CYP71D373. Product 10 structure was inferred, not fully confirmed. Mechanism likely proceeds via radical-mediated cyclization from 16-hydroxylated intermediate 7.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: bh2-7-derived line from S. miltiorrhiza var. alba
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream oxidation step in abietane diterpenoid/tanshinone pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms upstream precursor for downstream tanshinones
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive medicinal nor-diterpenoids with cardiovascular and cerebrovascular protective, antioxidant, antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, antitumor, and anti-HIV activities
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most highly expressed of the four clade I CYP71Ds in roots
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes hydroxylation at C20 of sugiol to form 20-hydroxysugiol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; upstream hydroxylase overlapping with CYP76AK1 activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes upstream C20 oxidation toward tanshinone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown of CYP71D clade; heterologous yeast expression and microsomal enzyme assay; scale-up reaction and NMR identification of 20-hydroxysugiol
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ATR1 (Arabidopsis NADPH-CYP reductase in yeast assay context)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; metabolomics (LC-qTOF-MS, GC-QqQ-MS); genome sequencing/assembly BioProject PRJNA682867
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71D411 is highly root-expressed and included in the RNAi-targeted clade associated with tanshinone reduction. 2. Biochemical assays showed CYP71D411 hydroxylates sugiol (3) to 20-hydroxysugiol (12). 3. This activity places CYP71D411 as an upstream C20 hydroxylase rather than a D-ring heterocyclase.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to enhance precursor oxidation and possibly increase pathway flux when combined with downstream D-ring-forming CYP71Ds
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function overlaps with previously characterized CYP76AK1; authors speculate CYP71D411 may have arisen to increase flux, but this is hypothetical.
